title: UX Widgets
html:
UX Widgets

Add to My Topics
Add selected topic and subtopicsAdd Topic & Subtopics with topic headAdd entire publication

Download PDF
Save selected topicSave selected topic and subtopicsSave entire publication
Share
Email LinkCopy Link
Feedback
  • Last UpdatedMai 15, 2026
    • Uniface
    • 10.4

Uniface UX provides a set of widgets that are implemented using the UX Widget Interface. This set is referred as UX Widgets. The sources of these widgets are available and allow changes and/or extensions to the widgets. In a default Uniface installation, you can find the sources in the <install>\uniface\webapps\uniface\common\ux folder.

UX widgets in Uniface UX exist at four levels:

  • Component - At the DSP Component level.
  • Entity - Data collections, with two sub-types:
    • Collection Widget - Manages the collection as a whole.
    • Occurrence Widget - Manages individual occurrences within the collection.
  • Field - Individual controls within an entity.

UX Widgets are developed based on Microsoft's Fluent Design System and most widgets are constructed by wrapping a web component from the Fluent library.

For more information on Fluent Design System, see the Fluent: Web component Explorer, Fluent: UI Web Components Overview, and Fluent: Icons sections on Fluent's website.

For more information on the Fast web components, as Fluent is based on Fast, see the FAST: Explorer and FAST: Documentation sections on FAST's website.

The set of UX Widgets supported are named with ux- as prefix , such as ux-TextField. For more details on the UX Widgets, see Widget Reference: Dynamic Server Pages.

CAUTION: The UX Widgets adhere to the UX Design System, while classic widgets do not. It is important to exercise caution when combining these widgets.

Widget sources

The widget source files, including JavaScript and CSS, are available in the official GitHub repository. You can download or explore them at:

https://github.com/RocketSoftware/uniface-ux/tree/main/src/ux/

Each widget has its own subdirectory under src/ux/.

Related Links
  • Widget Reference: Dynamic Server Pages

    Widgets in dynamic server pages (DSPs) determine the appearance and behavior of fields, labels, occurrences, and entities displayed in the client browser.

  • uxentlayout

    The uxentlayout provides two container components for managing Uniface entity data: CollectionLayout and OccurrenceLayout. These widgets work together to create structured, flexible layouts for displaying entity layout collections and individual occurrences, following the Fluent Design System.

  • uxheaderfooter

    The uxheaderfooter widget is a component-level widget to structure web page layouts into three semantic regions — header, main, and footer. The uxheaderfooter widget supports two configuration modes: a smart index‑based assignment that automatically maps children to header, main, and footer based on their order, and a name‑based assignment where developers explicitly place widgets using slot labels. The first mode offers effortless defaults for quick setups, while the second one provides precise, declarative control with warnings for invalid slot names.

  • uxcomplayout

    The uxcomplayout widget is a component‑level widget designed to arrange its top-level or direct child elements, such as entities and labels in a specific layout direction, either vertical or horizontal. This can be used both as a main DSP and contained DSP.

  • uxbutton

    The uxbutton widget is a UI button control. It allows an end user to invoke any kind of explicitly defined action on clicking or pressing of the button.

  • uxtextfield

    The uxtextfield widget is a single-line text input control. It allows entry of various formats of single-line text, including plain text, text according to a certain pattern, date, tel, email, password, and URL.

  • uxtextarea

    The uxtextarea widget is a multi-line text input control. It allows entry of various formats of multi-line text.

  • uxswitch

    The uxswitch widget is a toggle field control. It always contains a field value, even if it is not a valid Boolean. Unlike the checkbox widget, the uxswitch widget does not provide the indeterminate state, so null value or empty ("") value results in a format error.

  • uxselect

    The uxselect widget is a listbox control that contains a list of possible values. You can select one or more values in a uxselect widget.

  • uxradiogroup

    The uxradiogroup widget is a button input control. It allows users to select a single item from a short list of options to make a choice. You can use uxradiogroup in layouts where you want to add a list of options to make a choice and view all options at once.

  • uxplaintext

    The uxplaintext widget is a read-only control that displays field values only. The field is explicitly set to non-editable and cannot be modified by the user.

  • uxnumberfield

    The uxnumberfield widget is a number field input control. It supports only numeric values.

  • uxlistbox

    The uxlistbox widget is a control element that displays a list of items and allows the user to select a single option from a short list. You can use uxlistbox in data-entry constructions next to a radiogroup and/or a textfield.

  • uxdatagrid

    The uxdatagrid is an entity-level widget that shows tabular entity data as a set of rows and columns. The uxdatagrid allows users to quickly and easily look up values that indicate some kind of connection between different types of data, for example name of an employee in an organization and their salary, or a day of the week, or the timetable for a local swimming pool.

  • uxcheckbox

    The uxcheckbox widget is a Boolean input control. It indicates values of the Boolean field state.

Was this topic helpful?
LikeDislike
